data.title: U.S. Senate Negotiates DHS Budget Amid Potential Government Shutdown
data.description: The U.S. Senate is currently negotiating a compromise on the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) budget with both Democratic and Republican parties. According to PANews, even if the Senate separates the DHS budget from six spending bills and approves the other five, there is still a risk of a 78% government shutdown this Saturday. Differences between the Senate and House versions of the bill require the House to readjust the legislation and address the DHS budget separately. However, the House is not in session this week, preventing swift action. Additionally, disputes over the Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) have intensified partisan divisions. Senate Democrats have criticized ICE's enforcement methods and are calling for reforms to address public concerns over enforcement chaos.

data.title: Stablecoin Supply Exceeds $300 Billion Amid Shifting Blockchain Activity
data.description: ARK Invest's quarterly report indicates that the total stablecoin supply exceeded $300 billion by the end of last year. According to NS3.AI, growth slowed after a market shock in October. The report notes that while decentralized finance (DeFi) usage as a cryptocurrency payment and transaction layer increased in the fourth quarter, activity migrated across various blockchain networks. The Base network notably saw a 121% rise in stablecoin transaction volume, reaching around $3 trillion, surpassing Ethereum and Tron.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Investments Among South Korean Officials Revealed
data.description: A recent report highlights that 25% of senior public officials in South Korea's Lee Jae-myung administration have invested in cryptocurrencies. According to NS3.AI, Choi Dong-seok, who leads the Ministry of Personnel Management, disclosed holdings that include over 11 BTC and 5,000 XRP, with a total value of approximately $1.9 million. Another official, Kim Nam-kuk, reported substantial altcoin investments valued at around $882,000.

data.title: Tether Maintains Neutral Stance on Stablecoin Yield Debate
data.description: Tether CEO Paolo Ardoino has confirmed that the company will remain neutral in the ongoing debate over stablecoin yields. According to NS3.AI, Ardoino clarified that Tether does not offer any yield products, dismissing reports that suggested the company was aiding banks in regulating stablecoin yields. This stance highlights Tether's commitment to focusing on its core product without participating in discussions related to yield regulations.

data.title: U.S. Senate Republican Leader Expects Procedural Vote to Proceed as Scheduled
data.description: U.S. Senate Republican Leader John Thune has expressed confidence that the procedural vote scheduled for 11:30 a.m. will proceed as planned. According to Jin10, Thune mentioned that he has discussed strategies with House Speaker Mike Johnson to ensure the bill's passage in the House.

data.title: Mitsubishi UFJ Revises U.S. Federal Reserve Rate Cut Forecast
data.description: Mitsubishi UFJ has adjusted its forecast regarding the U.S. Federal Reserve's interest rate cuts for this year. According to ChainCatcher, the financial institution now anticipates the first rate cut to occur in April.

data.title: Iran reportedly to conduct live-fire military exercises in the Strait of Hormuz
data.description: According to CCTV News, Iran issued a VHF radio notification to passing ships on January 29th, announcing that it will conduct military exercises in the Strait of Hormuz from February 1st to 2nd, including live-fire drills. It is understood that these exercises may affect shipping activities through the Strait of Hormuz. Currently, Iranian officials have not publicly commented on the specific scale and arrangements of the exercises. (Cailian Press)

data.title: In the past 24 hours, the cryptocurrency market saw $798 million in liquidations across all platforms, affecting 212,000 individuals.
data.description: According to CoinGlass data, 212,177 people worldwide were liquidated in the past 24 hours, with a total liquidation amount of $798 million. Of this, $690 million were long positions liquidated and $108 million were short positions liquidated. The total liquidation amount in one hour was $314 million, in four hours it was $497 million, and in 12 hours it was $547 million. The largest single liquidation occurred on the Hyperliquid BTC-USD trading pair, worth $31.6446 million.

data.title: The "1011 Insider Whale" lost $53 million in two hours, and its long positions worth $704 million have now suffered a paper loss of $84 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, Garrett Jin, the "1011 insider whale," has seen his long positions, currently valued at $704 million, suffer a floating loss of $84 million since adding to his positions on January 28. Affected by tonight's market decline, this address lost $53 million within two hours.

data.title: U.S. Senate Republican Leader Thune Optimistic About Avoiding Government Shutdown
data.description: U.S. Senate Republican Leader John Thune expressed optimism about reaching an agreement to prevent a government shutdown. According to Jin10, Thune noted that the latest agreement includes temporary funding for the Department of Homeland Security. He also mentioned that the White House is collaborating with Democrats on the funding deal. Thune emphasized the importance of the House of Representatives returning to negotiations to help avert the shutdown.

data.title: U.S. House Republicans Unlikely to Reconvene This Weekend
data.description: The U.S. House of Representatives, led by the Republican leadership, is unlikely to reconvene this weekend, according to Punchbowl. Attendance is expected to be low. Although a final decision has not been made, the House is scheduled to reconvene on Monday.

data.title: Onyxcoin Faces Uncertain Future Amid Price Breakout
data.description: Onyxcoin (XCN) has recently attempted a 28% price increase, but concerns about its sustainability persist. According to NS3.AI, long-term holders have been consistently selling, indicating a decline in investor confidence. Additionally, the Chaikin Money Flow indicator reveals ongoing capital outflows. For Onyxcoin to mitigate downside risks, which could see prices fall to $0.0062 or lower, it must break and maintain a position above the critical resistance level of $0.0077.

data.title: Swiss Franc Hits 11-Year High Against US Dollar Amid Global Uncertainty
data.description: The Swiss franc has reached an 11-year high against the US dollar, marking a 3.5% increase year-to-date and a 12% rise annually. According to NS3.AI, this surge is attributed to geopolitical tensions and global financial uncertainty, reinforcing the franc's status as a safe-haven asset. Despite its strength, the Swiss National Bank faces challenges in managing the currency's appreciation while maintaining economic stability.

data.title: Brother Machi lost $2 million in a single day, and his long positions were liquidated again.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, as the market declined, Machi Big Brother lost $2 million in a single day. His funds deposited with Hyperliquid were liquidated again.

data.title: Trend Research deposited 36.39 million USDT as margin with Aave and is currently experiencing a paper loss of $179 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, the price of ETH briefly fell to $2805. Trend Research (0xE5C...4e4c) withdrew 36.39 million USDT from Binance 15 minutes ago and deposited them in batches into Aave as margin. Currently, this address holds 661,272.65 ETH, with a holding cost of approximately $3104.36, resulting in a paper loss of $179 million.

data.title: Ethereum Foundation and Vitalik to Establish $220 Million Security Fund
data.description: Market reports indicate that the Ethereum Foundation and Vitalik Buterin are set to establish a $220 million security fund. According to Odaily, this initiative will be funded by "TheDAO." The fund aims to enhance security measures within the Ethereum ecosystem.

data.title: Silver Prices Experience Significant Decline
data.description: Silver prices have seen a notable drop, with spot silver briefly falling below $107 per ounce, marking an 8.81% decrease within the day. According to BlockBeats, New York silver futures also experienced a sharp decline of 6.00%, currently trading at $106.71 per ounce.

data.title: Michael Grimes to Depart U.S. Commerce Department
data.description: Michael Grimes is set to leave the U.S. Commerce Department, where he managed the government's venture capital division. According to Jin10, Grimes played a significant role in overseeing investments and fostering innovation within the department. His departure marks a notable change in the leadership of the venture capital sector within the government.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 85,000 USDT with a 5.09% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Jan 29, 2026, 15:40 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 85,000 USDT and is now trading at 84,888.007813 USDT, with a narrowed 5.09% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Crypto stocks fell sharply during trading in the US, with Strategy (MSTR) down 7.86%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, US stocks fell across the board during trading, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average down 0.38%, the S&P 500 down 0.94%, and the Nasdaq Composite down 1.81%. The cryptocurrency sector saw further declines, with Bitmine down 8.57%, Strategy (MSTR) down 7.86%, Circle down 7.66%, Solana down 6.54%, SharpLink down 5.82%, and Coinbase down 5.24%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stocks and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.
